name: BloodHound / SharpHound
categoryDescription: Commonly abused tools that leave NO Windows registry artifacts. These tools are typically single executables, portable builds, or in-memory frameworks. Detection depends entirely on non-registry evidence: process execution logs, prefetch, ShimCache/Amcache, BAM/DAM, network traffic, and memory forensics. Includes detection notes for each tool.
description: Active Directory attack path mapping tool. SharpHound is the C# data collector binary. Runs from disk or memory, creates ZIP output with AD relationship data. No installer, no registry.
forensic_value: No registry artifacts. LDAP enumeration volume and pattern is primary detection signal. Look for large numbers of LDAP queries from non-DC systems to DCs in short timeframes.
